name: tlssled
title: tlssled

commands.name: tlssled
commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# tlssled 192.168.1.1 443

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Evaluates the security of a target SSL/TLS (HTTPS) server
TLSSLed is a Linux shell script whose purpose is to evaluate
the security of a target SSL/TLS (HTTPS) web server
implementation. It is based on sslscan, a thorough SSL/TLS
scanner that is based on the openssl library, and on the
"openssl s_client" command line tool. The current tests
include checking if the target supports the SSLv2 protocol,
the NULL cipher, weak ciphers based on their key length (40
or 56 bits), the availability of strong ciphers (like AES),
if the digital certificate is MD5 signed, and the current
SSL/TLS renegotiation capabilities.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tlssled because ### tlssled.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tlssled with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tlssled --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### tlssled.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install tlssled
